section: Chapter 8: A Shirt in the Market

questions.question: What is the first stage in the production of a shirt?
questions.explanation: Cotton is the raw material used to make cloth and garments.

questions.question: What is the role of spinning mills?
questions.explanation: Spinning mills process raw cotton and turn it into yarn used for weaving cloth.

questions.question: What does weaving mean?
questions.explanation: Yarn produced in spinning mills is woven into fabric in textile units.

questions.question: Who stitches shirts in the production chain?
questions.explanation: Workers in garment factories cut and stitch cloth to make shirts.

questions.question: Who connects farmers, factories, and markets in the production chain?
questions.explanation: Traders buy goods from producers and sell them to factories or companies.

questions.question: What is subcontracting?
questions.correct_answer: When large companies give production work to smaller factories
questions.explanation: Subcontracting allows companies to reduce costs and increase production.

questions.question: Why do garment workers often earn low wages?
questions.correct_answer: Because they work for contractors or small factories under big companies
questions.explanation: The profits are mostly earned by big companies and retailers.

questions.question: What is meant by the production chain?
questions.correct_answer: The sequence of steps involved in producing and selling a product
questions.explanation: Many people and processes are involved from raw material to final sale.

questions.question: Why are some shirts exported to other countries?
questions.explanation: Garment industries produce clothing for global markets.

questions.question: Who usually earns the highest profit in the shirt market?
questions.explanation: They sell finished products at much higher prices than the production cost.

notes.title: Production Chain
notes.content: A process involving cotton farming, spinning, weaving, stitching, and selling shirts.

notes.title: Garment Industry
notes.content: Factories and workers involved in making clothing from cloth.

notes.title: Subcontracting System
notes.content: Large companies outsource production work to smaller factories or workshops.

notes.title: Global Market
notes.content: Garments produced in one country are often sold in other countries through export companies.

notes.title: Profit Distribution
notes.content: Different people earn different profits in the production chain, with brands earning the most.

facts.fact: A single shirt passes through many hands before reaching the customer.
Cotton is the main raw material used to make most shirts.
Garment workers often work long hours but receive low wages.
Large clothing brands sell shirts at prices much higher than production costs.
